supporting_evidence.Observation: Compression analysis detected image processing characteristics that may result from resizing, export, social-media transmission or benign editing. These findings should be interpreted together with other forensic evidence.
Frequency-domain analysis detected minimal manipulation signals, supporting image authenticity.

counter_evidence.Observation: Metadata is consistent with a typical camera-generated image.
counter_evidence.Reason: This forensic engine produced a low manipulation signal, supporting image authenticity and reducing the overall confidence in AI-generated manipulation.

evidence_fusion_summary: Only one forensic engine produced a strong manipulation signal. Independent corroboration was limited.
The AI classifier was the only strong forensic indicator. Adaptive evidence weighting reduced its influence because other forensic engines did not provide sufficient corroboration.
The evidence is more consistent with normal digital enhancement such as professional retouching, export or image compression than malicious AI-generated manipulation.
Final Banking Decision: GENUINE - DIGITALLY ENHANCED.

Recommended Action: The image appears authentic but contains signs of normal digital enhancement (for example, studio retouching or compression). Continue with standard verification while recording the enhancement finding for audit purposes..
